methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $850M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Dusty Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Tessa Lau and Philipp Herget that has raised $70M+ to date. The comparable universe comprises 9 private companies and 1 public company across the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including peers such as Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Given data constraints, only one valuation method proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the company's $70M+ total raised as a proxy baseline ($850M implied reference value) and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding an implied valuation of $1.7B. This method reflects expected value accretion between funding stages but relies on the assumption that total capital raised correlates meaningfully to enterprise value.

Peer-median comparison was not feasible; while seven same-sector peers exist, insufficient overlap was found among same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier companies to support a statistically reliable benchmark. The triangulated range derived from available analysis spans $1.4B to $2.0B, with a median point of $1.7B. Key uncertainties include the stability of stage-progression multipliers in the robotics sector, the company's unit economics relative to peers, and competitive positioning within a fragmented manufacturing automation market. Additional financial data and clearer peer segmentation would strengthen conviction in this range.
